# Survey section — Design-space exploration is the shared design vehicle across CPU perf-modeling and edge-LLM accelerator work: one side makes each DSE point cheap to evaluate, the other searches a hardware/dataflow space.

*Generated from committed graph state. Every substantive sentence traces to a claim id; extraction claims further cite a source passage (C1/traceability DoD).*

**Thesis (C-SYNTH-1).** Design-space exploration is the shared design vehicle across CPU perf-modeling and edge-LLM accelerator work: one side makes each DSE point cheap to evaluate, the other searches a hardware/dataflow space. *(inference; confidence 0.6)*

**Supporting evidence:**

- The analytical-ML surrogate is >5 orders of magnitude faster than a cycle-level simulator at ~2% average CPI error. *(C-CONCORDE-3, measured)* — source [arxiv:2503.23076 p4]: "Experiments show that Concorde is more than five orders of magnitude faster than a reference cycle-level simulator, with about 2% average Cycles-Per-Instruction (CPI) prediction error across a range of SPEC, open-source, and proprietary benchmarks."
- A hybrid-bonding heterogeneous architecture plus a data-centric dataflow abstraction, searched by a DSE framework, co-explores hardware and dataflow. *(C-H2LLM-2, inferred)* — source [doi:10.1145/3695053.3731008 p3]: "To balance the trade-off between computation capacity and bandwidth intrinsic to hybrid-bonding technology, we propose H2-LLM's architecture and extract its architecture design space."
